/**
 * Barrel re-export of the focused DOM-builder modules.
 *
 * The former monolith was split into domain modules under `src/app/`:
 *  - `./menus.js`       — menu lifecycle, viewport-edge positioning, per-mount
 *                         menu state, the per-row ⋮ menu, and the directory
 *                         context menu.
 *  - `./rows.js`        — browse / search / parent table-row builders.
 *  - `./breadcrumb.js`  — breadcrumb populator and in-app navigation links.
 *  - `./icons.js`       — Bootstrap Icons glyph and action-button builders.
 *  - `./tables.js`      — generic results-table skeleton builder.
 *
 * This barrel exists so existing `import ... from './dom-builders'` paths
 * (notably the test suites, which are not updated when the split lands) keep
 * resolving. New production code should import from the focused modules
 * directly.
 */
export * from './menus.js';
export * from './rows.js';
export * from './breadcrumb.js';
export * from './icons.js';
export * from './tables.js';

/**
 * SPA entry — wires `api`, `router`, and the DOM into a file-browser UI
 * rendered inside a native `<dialog>` widget.
 *
 * This module is the thin entry point: it builds the DOM imperatively, wires
 * toolbar/dialog events, binds the resulting element refs into the render
 * orchestrator (which owns the route dispatch and render lifecycle), and kicks
 * off the initial render. The rendering logic itself lives in the focused
 * modules under `./app/`:
 *
 *   - `./app/context.js`             — shared API client, DOM refs, render hook
 *   - `./app/dom-builders.js`        — table/breadcrumb/row/action-button builders
 *   - `./app/render-browse.js`       — browse-result rendering
 *   - `./app/render-search.js`       — search-result rendering
 *   - `./app/toolbar-handlers.js`    — search + upload-into-folder handlers
 *   - `./app/render-orchestrator.js` — route dispatch + render lifecycle
 *
 * Structure (built imperatively via `document.createElement`):
 *
 *   .file-browser-host   — primary chromeless surface; hosts the widget while
 *                          the dialog is closed (embedded view)
 *   <button class="trigger">Browse Files</button>
 *   <dialog class="browser-dialog">  — frame + titlebar (dialog-only chrome)
 *     .dialog-header     — title <span> + Close icon <button>
 *     .dialog-body        — empty slot; the shared widget moves here on open

[181 more lines in file. Use offset=26 to continue.]

Let me write up the final review. The implementation is approved overall with minor findings about stale references in related files (app.ts, app.css, test file headers). These are out of the strict scope of the task (which focuses on splitting dom-builders.ts and updating its direct importers), so they could be considered WONTFIX per rule #2. But they're directly related to the split and worth noting.

I'll mark the implementation as approved with these minor notes.

content.text:
Based on my review, the implementation correctly splits the monolith into the five focused domain modules as specified, preserves all the interesting features (computeMenuPosition flip/clamp math, WAI-ARIA keyboard contract, single-open-menu invariant, HTML injection prevention), updates all production-code imports to direct module paths, and keeps the barrel for test compatibility (an approach explicitly allowed by the task). All 439 tests pass and `tsc --noEmit` exits cleanly.

{"approved": true, "feedback": "The split is complete and correct. All five suggested modules were created with the right contents: menus.ts (MenuAnchor, MENU_EDGE_MARGIN, computeMenuPosition, getViewportSize, createMenu, createMenuState/MenuState, makeRowMenu, makeDirMenu, setupDirContextMenu), rows.ts (formatSizeColumn, makeBrowseRow, makeSearchRow, makeParentRow, navigateRowOnClick), breadcrumb.ts (renderBreadcrumb, makeNavLink), icons.ts (makeIcon, makeActionButton), and tables.ts (buildTable). Internal helpers (createMenu, makeDirMenu, getViewportSize, formatSizeColumn, navigateRowOnClick) correctly remain un-exported; makeRowMenu is necessarily exported now because rows.ts consumes it across the module boundary (it was module-private in the monolith). Production code (render-browse.ts, render-search.ts, render-orchestrator.ts) was updated to use DIRECT imports from the new modules, while the barrel is kept solely for the test suites — exactly the preferred pattern called out by the task. The barrel re-exports `*` from each new module so no originally-exported symbol is lost. All four preserved features are intact and verified: computeMenuPosition is pure and unchanged; the WAI-ARIA keyboard nav (ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Home/End with wrapping, Escape, outside-click) is in createMenu; the single-open invariant is enforced via closeAllRowMenus() in openMenu; all user-controlled strings still go through textContent/element creation. Fresh, non-temporal JSDoc was written for every new module and every exported symbol. `npm test` reports 439 passed / 3 skipped across 15 files, and `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0 (the npm run build failure is purely the unrelated copy-assets step — bootstrap-icons isn't installed in this worktree). The only things I noted are minor stale doc references in files outside the split's direct scope (see issues); none of them affect correctness, behavior, or test results.", "issues": [{"file": "src/app.ts", "description": "Stale JSDoc: line 12 still lists `./app/dom-builders.js — table/breadcrumb/row/action-button builders`. Since dom-builders.js is now just a re-export barrel, this description is misleading. Ideally it should either describe dom-builders.js as the barrel or be replaced with entries for the new focused modules (menus/rows/breadcrumb/icons/tables). Strictly outside the task's stated scope (it's not an importer that needs updating), so this is a cosmetic doc-drift note.", "severity": "minor"}, {"file": "src/app.css", "description": "Two CSS comments reference functions by their old location: line 261 says `setupDirContextMenu in dom-builders.ts` (now in menus.ts) and line 357 says `navigateRowOnClick in dom-builders.ts` (now in rows.ts). Cosmetic doc drift only; outside the strict task scope.", "severity": "minor"}, {"file": "src/app/dom-builders.test.ts", "description": "Header comment says `Tests for the row-menu placement helpers in dom-builders.ts`. The helpers now live in menus.ts (computeMenuPosition, MENU_EDGE_MARGIN) and icons.ts (makeIcon, makeActionButton), reached via the barrel. The test still imports from `./dom-builders` so it isn't wrong, but the file pointer is stale. Outside the strict task scope (the test file is intentionally not updated when keeping the barrel).", "severity": "minor"}, {"file": "src/app/dom-builders.hardening.test.ts", "description": "Header says `Hardening tests for src/app/dom-builders.ts`. The behavior under test now lives in menus.ts/rows.ts/icons.ts. Same minor staleness as above; tests still pass unchanged via the barrel.", "severity": "minor"}]}
